title: Delicious Sweet Venezuelan Coconut Empanadas
description: Sweet and crumbly empanadas filled with coconut and candied peel, perfect for a snack or dessert. This halal-friendly recipe is made in a kazan cauldron.
instructions: Preheat the kazan cauldron over medium heat.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, confectioners sugar, and cold butter, and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add the shredded coconut, candied peel, eggs, coconut milk, and salt to the bowl, and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces, and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, and place a tablespoon of coconut filling in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas in the preheated kazan cauldron, and cook for 10 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
